strengths: Immediately reached for a hash set for O(n) duplicate detection — no prompting needed
Correctly identified the O(n) space tradeoff without being asked
concerns: Did not consider the sorted array optimization when follow-up was posed — only said 'you could use binary search' without elaborating
problem_solving.evidence: Candidate said: 'I'd iterate through the array, store each element in a hash set, and if I see one that's already in the set, that's my duplicate. Time is O(n), space is O(n).'

strengths: Correctly explained hash map use case for frequency counting
Knew that hash maps have O(1) average lookup
concerns: Could not explain collision handling — said 'I think it handles it automatically' without knowing about chaining or open addressing
Limited exposure to trees — said 'I know what they are but haven't used them in a project'
data_structures.evidence: When asked about collisions: 'I think the language handles it automatically, I haven't had to think about that.'

strengths: Described a systematic approach: reproduce → isolate → check logs → hypothesis
Mentioned checking environment differences between local and production
Referenced using a debugger breakpoint rather than relying solely on console.log
concerns: Did not mention monitoring tools or error tracking services (Sentry, etc.)
debugging_approach.evidence: Candidate said: 'First thing I'd do is try to reproduce it locally with the same data. If I can't, I'd look at the environment — maybe it's a config variable or a dependency version that differs.'

strengths: Correctly identified that 'clean code means readable by someone else in 6 months'
Showed awareness of naming conventions and avoiding magic numbers
concerns: Could not give a concrete example of pushing back in a code review — said 'I usually just go along with reviewer comments'
Communication was clear but answers were surface-level — did not demonstrate SOLID principles or pattern awareness
code_quality_and_communication.evidence: On clean code: 'I think it means code that someone else can read without asking you what it does. Good variable names, no magic numbers, that kind of thing.'

summary: A capable junior candidate with solid algorithmic instincts and a disciplined debugging mindset. Data structure depth is adequate but shallow on internals. Communicates clearly but stays at surface level on software engineering principles. Would be productive with mentorship on a team with strong code review culture.
notable_positives: Unprompted mention of space-time tradeoff — shows she's thinking beyond just 'making it work'
Debugging approach was structured and showed production awareness — rare for a junior
